title: Flutter App Not Connected to Backend
description: Flutter frontend must establish HTTP connections to backend API at /api/v1/* endpoints. Without this integration, Phase 5 testing cannot proceed.

correction_steps.details: Verify mobile/ folder contains Flutter project with pubspec.yaml, lib/, test/ directories
Update pubspec.yaml: add 'http: ^1.1.0' or 'dio: ^5.3.0' for HTTP requests. Run 'flutter pub get'
Create lib/services/api_service.dart with methods for: register(), login(), createTranslation(), getTranslations(). Base URL: http://localhost:4000/api/v1/ (dev) or production URL

1) POST /api/v1/auth/register with {email, password}. 2) POST /api/v1/auth/login with {email, password}. 3) Store JWT token in secure storage (flutter_secure_storage). 4) Include Bearer token in all subsequent requests: Authorization: Bearer {token}

1) POST /api/v1/translations with {signText, confidence, translatedText}. 2) GET /api/v1/translations with pagination (page, limit). 3) Handle pagination: parse response with total_pages, current_page. 4) Format requests with Content-Type: application/json

Catch and handle error responses: { code: 'ERROR_TYPE', message: string, timestamp: ISO-8601 }. Specific handling: 400=validation error, 401=auth error (redirect to login), 500=server error (show user message)
Test endpoints with: 1) Health check: GET /api/v1/health. 2) Successful registration. 3) Successful login and token storage. 4) POST translation with valid token. 5) GET translations list.

validation: All Flutter API calls return 2xx responses; tokens persist across app restarts; JWT refresh works on 401
error_handling: If connection fails: verify backend running on localhost:4000; check CORS allowed origins; inspect network logs in Flutter DevTools

title: ML Model Not Connected to Backend
description: TensorFlow Lite model inference results must be sent to backend for storage. Without this integration, ML-backend feedback loop cannot be tested.
blocking_items: Cannot test end-to-end sign language workflow

correction_steps.details: Check ml/ folder for model file (model.tflite or equivalent); verify ml/ has model loading scripts; ensure model accepts video/image input and outputs class predictions

Create lib/services/ml_service.dart that: 1) Loads TensorFlow Lite model using tflite_flutter package. 2) Processes camera frames. 3) Returns predictions with confidence scores (0-1). 4) Handles model errors gracefully

On each inference result: 1) Get predicted sign class. 2) Get confidence score. 3) Determine translation text from class. 4) Call api_service.createTranslation(signText, confidence, translation). 5) Update UI with result
Only send to backend if confidence > 0.7 (configurable). Log rejected low-confidence predictions. This prevents backend from storing poor quality results.
Before sending to backend, validate: 1) signText non-empty. 2) confidence in range [0, 1]. 3) translatedText non-empty. 4) User authenticated (has valid token)
If backend POST fails: 1) Log error with details. 2) Retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff. 3) Show user offline indicator. 4) Queue failed translations for retry when online

1) Start app, login. 2) Point camera at sign language. 3) Get inference result. 4) Verify POST to /api/v1/translations succeeds. 5) Verify result appears in GET /api/v1/translations list. 6) Check backend logs for stored data

validation: ML inference results successfully posted to /api/v1/translations; translations persistent in backend; confidence scores stored correctly
error_handling: If ML model fails to load: verify model file location; check tflite_flutter package version compatibility. If API POST fails: check network connectivity; verify authentication token valid

title: Rate Limiting Blocks Testing
description: Current rate limiting (5 requests/15min for auth, 10/hour for register) blocks integration testing. Need to temporarily reduce limits for testing.
blocking_items: CI/CD pipeline will fail on rate limits

correction_steps.details: Create backend/.env.test with relaxed rate limits: API_RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS=10000, API_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS=60000 (1 min). This allows unlimited testing
Update backend/src/middleware/security.js to check NODE_ENV: if NODE_ENV=test, use high thresholds; if NODE_ENV=production, use strict limits from .env
Create jest.config.js setupEnv to set NODE_ENV=test before tests run. Update package.json scripts: 'test': 'NODE_ENV=test jest', 'test:dev': 'NODE_ENV=development node scripts/test_*.js'
Add NODE_ENV=test to all test scripts in backend/scripts/test_*.js. Add comment: '# Run with npm test for rate-limited testing' or use .env.test automatically
Update README: 'Development mode: NODE_ENV=development uses loose limits. Test mode: NODE_ENV=test has no rate limiting. Production mode: strict limits enforced'
1) Start backend with NODE_ENV=test. 2) Run test scripts; confirm no 429 errors. 3) Verify production config still enforces rate limits when NODE_ENV=production

validation: Test execution completes without 429 rate limit errors; production environment still enforces strict limits
error_handling: If tests still hit rate limits: increase thresholds further; check for running test instances; verify NODE_ENV variable set correctly
deliverables: Test execution without rate limit errors

title: Database Configuration for Testing and Production
description: In-memory database works for dev but needs proper configuration for persistent testing and production deployment.
blocking_items: Data not persistent between test runs

correction_steps.details: 1) Create account at mongodb.com/cloud. 2) Create cluster (free tier available). 3) Add database user (username, strong password). 4) Add IP whitelist (0.0.0.0/0 for dev, specific IPs for prod). 5) Get connection string
Update backend/.env: USE_IN_MEMORY_DB=false (leave true for dev). Add MONGODB_URI=mongodb+srv://user:pass@cluster.mongodb.net/asl_live_detection?retryWrites=true&w=majority
Create separate MongoDB database for testing (e.g., 'asl_live_detection_test'). Add MONGODB_TEST_URI to .env.test. Jest setup: clear test database before/after each test suite
Edit backend/src/db.js: if NODE_ENV=test, use MONGODB_TEST_URI; if USE_IN_MEMORY_DB=true, use memory database; otherwise use MONGODB_URI
Create backend/scripts/db-setup.js: sets up indexes, creates collections, optionally seeds test data. Run once after connecting to new database
Update backend/src/routes/health.js to report database connection status; include replica set info if applicable
Create DEPLOYMENT.md with instructions: local dev (in-memory), test (MongoDB test instance), production (Atlas or self-hosted). Include connection troubleshooting

1) Start with in-memory (USE_IN_MEMORY_DB=true). 2) Create user, add translation. 3) Restart app, verify data lost (expected). 4) Switch to MongoDB, repeat, verify data persists. 5) Run tests with test database

validation: Data persists across restarts; test database isolates test data from production; all CRUD operations work with both in-memory and MongoDB
error_handling: If MongoDB connection fails: verify connection string; check IP whitelist; ensure network connectivity. If test database contaminated: clear collections before test run
dependencies: MongoDB Atlas account or local MongoDB instance
